changeReason: New translation uses 'pat' instead of 'Tap' for ひた, conveying softer, more stealthy footsteps matching the Japanese onomatopoeia.

TextENGNew: Those stifled footsteps came to a halt just outside my door.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: A silence hung in the air... as though someone were listening to see if I was inside.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...And of all things, I was still trapped in the haze of shallow sleep.
changeReason: New translation uses 'shallow sleep' instead of 'restless slumber,' more accurately reflecting 浅い睡眠 (light/shallow sleep).

TextENGNew: My consciousness was gradually sharpening, but my body refused to follow.
...Even though danger lurked just beyond the door, a single barrier away...@k I couldn't move, as if struck by sleep paralysis.
changeReason: New translation specifically references 'sleep paralysis' for 金縛り, which is the precise cultural meaning of the Japanese term.

TextENGNew: ......That was, without question, pure terror.
Please......just go away...
Come on, why won't my body wake up?@k If someone barged into my room right now......!!
``UwaaAAAAaaAAAH!!!!``
I bolted upright and hurled my blankets at my mom as she opened the door!!
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``Wha--!@k@vS01/00/122700024. What is it, Keiichi?!@k@vS01/00/122700025. What's wrong?``
I'd thought it was still one or two in the morning.
But a hollow morning light was already spilling through the gap in the curtains.
......It was morning, though it didn't feel real at all.
Yesterday, I must have... fallen asleep right after that.
......If so, I should have gotten a full ten hours of sleep......
...But it certainly didn't feel that way.
My internal clock was completely thrown off, and my sense of balance felt dulled.
I even felt feverish, making it painfully clear that I was far from healthy...
``...How are you, Keiichi?@k@vS01/00/122700027. How's your body feeling?@k@vS01/00/122700028. Think you can make it to school?``
......I was in no condition to go to school... no, my mental state wouldn't allow it.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Yesterday's terror crept back, clinging to me like a damp cloth...
If I had just... swallowed that needle as it was... what would have happened...?
Or if it had stabbed into the inside of my mouth, piercing my tongue...?
The intent to harm was unmistakable, but even so... it wasn't meant to be lethal.
changeReason: Both say intent was present but not fully lethal; 'not all it was' vs 'not meant to be lethal' convey same idea.

TextENGNew: If they truly wanted to kill me, they'd use a more reliable method.@k They wouldn't resort to something as uncertain as making me swallow a needle.
......In other words......as much as I didn't want to believe it......@k even going this far was... merely a threat... from Rena and Mion.
Aren't you glad you didn't die...? But next time, we'll use a surer method...?
Something like that.
Sending a razor blade inside a letter... compared to this, that would be nothing more than a prank...
``...Did you go to the clinic properly?@k@vS01/00/122700030. Did you take your medicine?``
changeReason: New translation uses 'clinic' instead of 'hospital,' better reflecting the rural medical setting.

TextENGNew: ``Mm... yeah. More or less...``
Something about my mom's skeptical expression rubbed me the wrong way.
It was because... her displeasure at her son missing two days of school in a row was far more visible than any concern for him being ill.
...Sure, I'd had a mental shock, but... I wasn't actually sick or anything.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``Once you break your daily rhythm, it's hard to get it back.@k@vS01/00/122700032. Come on, up and at 'em!@k@vS01/00/122700033. They say illness starts in the mind, you know.``
That was a line I'd heard since I was little.
I'd been awarded perfect attendance when I graduated elementary school... but it wasn't because I was especially robust.
``Come on, go wash your face.@k@vS01/00/122700035. Breakfast is ready.@k@vS01/00/122700036. ...There's not much time left before Rena-chan comes for you.``
Mom's tone left no room for argument, so I gave up on faking sick for a second day.

``Oh, right. ...Keiichi, were you the one who threw ohagi at the living room wall?@k@vS01/00/122700038. You shouldn't do things like that!@k@vS01/00/122700039. Your father was quite upset, you know?``
changeReason: New translation corrects 'bean paste' to 'ohagi,' accurately naming the food item from the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ......I didn't feel the slightest guilt about it, so I didn't bother responding.
Mom didn't press the matter any further either.
Once she confirmed I was sluggishly crawling out of bed, she headed back downstairs.
Mion's parting words from yesterday -- 'You'd better not skip school tomorrow, okay?' -- resurfaced in my mind, sticky and clinging.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: What did she mean by that...?
It was obvious without even thinking about it.@k ...She was telling me: don't be absent again.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Taking it a step further... she was telling me to live my life as if nothing had happened.
If I showed any sign that something was off, it would look suspicious.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: And if I acted strange... someone like Ooishi-san, for instance, might pick up on 'something being wrong.'
In other words, ...even if I kept my mouth shut, if I behaved any differently from normal,@k it would end up communicating something to those 'undesirable' people...
And it seemed the girls had no intention of allowing even that...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: So then... if I just went on living as normal... they wouldn't harm me... was that the deal...?
The gloom that had weighed on me until yesterday was fading at an almost unsettling rate... It was a tempting deal.
changeReason: Old translation contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new translation accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: All I had to do was cleanly forget everything I'd seen and heard these past few days... and I could return to my everyday life.
``Th... There's no way... something that convenient could...``
I swallowed the saliva that had pooled in my mouth...
...The idea I'd almost dismissed... I reconsidered, taking it one step further...
Mion was probably... a good person who looked out for her friends.
...She was giving me, someone who'd unknowingly broken some rule of theirs... a second chance.
What I'd done... must have truly been something unforgivable...
But Mion had given me a chance.
...She was saying that if I forgot everything and went on living as I had before, she'd forgive me...
``Keiichi~!@k@vS01/00/122700041. Your breakfast is getting cold!@k@vS01/00/122700042. Hurry up and come down~!@k@vS01/00/122700043. If you don't hurry, Rena-chan will be here!``
``Oh... I-I'm coming!``
I crammed my textbooks haphazardly into my bag and hurried downstairs.
I picked listlessly at a lackluster breakfast with my chopsticks.
There was almost no time left. I'd already missed the time I usually met up with Rena.
Judging by yesterday... she'd probably come to get me within five minutes.
...I had to be ready to leave for school by then...
Forget everything that happened until yesterday...
Forget it all and just live normally...
Normally, I'd already be at the place where I meet Rena...
...Of all days, today the rice was dry and hard to swallow.
Ding-dong!
I flinched and dropped my chopsticks.@k ...It was the doorbell announcing Rena's arrival.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Mom urged me to hurry.
``See? Rena-chan's already here...!@k@vS01/00/122700045. Hurry, hurry!``
My mother's cheerful smile and the shadow on my face couldn't have been more different.
Honestly, I felt a deep reluctance to greet the Rena waiting beyond the front door.
...Would the Rena on the other side of that door... be the Rena I knew?
But I couldn't keep her waiting...@k I had to act like I always did......
``Good morning~!``
The morning greeting that filtered through the doorway was perfectly refreshing.
``...Keiichi-kun was running late, so I came to check on him... will he be okay today, I wonder? ...I wonder?``
The way Rena showed concern was undoubtedly the Rena I knew.
...But that was surely contingent on me playing my part as usual.
Forget everything up until yesterday... as though nothing had happened...
Forget the brutal murder at the dam construction site.@k Forget the mysterious deaths that occurred every year after.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...The falls, the illness, the suicide, the bludgeoning, the disappearance -- forget it all. Every last bit.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Forget that Rena was frightening. Forget that Mion was frightening.@k Forget everything.@k Forget the ohagi too.@k Forget, forget, forget.
changeReason: New translation corrects 'mochi' to 'ohagi,' naming the specific Japanese confection in the scene.

TextENGNew: Rena asked once more, just to make sure.
``...Can you make it to school?``
``Ah......yeah. ...I'm fine.``
``That's great!@k@vS01/02/120200166. Then let's go! Mii-chan's waiting!``
Rena gave me her usual bright smile.
I couldn't detect the slightest hint of duplicity in her expression.@k ...The tension drained from me, and I realized I felt relieved...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``And then Satoko-chan, she got all stubborn about it and insisted she could definitely do it~!``
As we walked, Rena chatted about all sorts of things, even more than usual.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``...Hm. ...And then?``
``Satoko-chan's so clumsy, she couldn't do it no matter how many times she tried! ...Hau, she was so cuuuute...``
Everything Rena talked about was trivial, and all I had to do was nod along or laugh occasionally. It was an easy, relaxed conversation.
A neighbor we were passing called out to us.
``Oh my, Keiichi-chan and Rena-chan, aren't you running a little late today~?@k@vS01/00/villaA02008. Mion-chan said she was going on ahead!``
``Oh no! Is Mii-chan mad, I wonder?@k@vS01/02/120200170. ...I wonder?!@k@vS01/02/120200171. Let's hurry, Keiichi-kun!``
After greeting our neighbor with a smile, Rena turned back to me and playfully stuck out her tongue.
That little gesture was infectious, and I felt my cheeks relax into a natural smile.
``...Ah, Keiichi-kun smiled.``
``Huh? ...Wh-what?``
Rena stopped walking and gazed at me intently.
``...You seemed down again this morning, Keiichi-kun, so I thought maybe your cold still hadn't gone away... but it looks like you're okay now. ...Okay!``
Smiling softly, she gave my cheek a little poke.
It was an honest, refreshing smile, without a trace of pretense.
...Hey, Maebara Keiichi.
...Even after seeing that smile, are you still going to doubt Rena...?
Maybe these past few days I'd just been bedridden with a terrible fever, seeing hallucinations that never really happened?
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ......I couldn't help but genuinely wish that were the case.
If God would grant me just one wish...@k There was only one thing I'd ask for.
These past several days of events.
...If I had to be more specific, everything from the night of the Watanagashi until last night... I wanted all of it to have never happened.
How many times had I wished for that in these past few days......
If Rena could just keep smiling like this forever... maybe that wish could come true.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: So I wanted Rena to keep smiling, always.@k ...Always... keep smiling...
``So, Keiichi-kun.@k@|@y@vS01/02/120200175. Yesterday's ohagi... did you eat them properly...?``
changeReason: New translation corrects 'mochi' to 'ohagi,' naming the specific Japanese confection in the scene.

TextENGNew: That fragile wish of mine was dashed in an instant.
My heart lurched, and the gentle morning air froze solid in a heartbeat.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Rena's smile... was her usual smile, and her eyes appeared gentle, the same as always...
Yesterday's ohagi... did you eat them properly...?
changeReason: New translation corrects 'mochi' to 'ohagi,' naming the specific Japanese confection in the scene.

TextENGNew: ...Of course she wasn't asking at face value.
What Rena really meant was... 'Did our message get through to you?' ...Is that what she was trying to say...?
Rena stopped walking and peered deeply into my eyes as I hesitated to answer.
...Don't... hesitate, Maebara Keiichi.
......Rena was acting just like she always did, wasn't she?
I had to respond in kind.@k ......And naturally, at that...!
But before I knew it, my throat and lips had gone bone-dry, my upper and lower lips sealed tight against each other...
Answer her quickly, Keiichi...
Not that much time had passed yet.@k I could still... keep the conversation flowing naturally...
Whatever else... I had to find the words......quickly......
Rena playfully mimicked the sound I'd finally managed to squeeze out.
Rena's reaction was perfectly normal.@k ......The pause hadn't been as long as I'd feared.
Somehow, I forced out the rest of my reply.
``Th-... They were good...!``
But despite my desperate effort, Rena's expression neither brightened nor clouded.
For an instant, I panicked, thinking I'd given the wrong answer.
But after a few moments, Rena's face softened into that familiar smile, and she laughed with a lilting voice that seemed to clear the morning air itself.
It was the kind of laugh that pulled you in... so I found myself laughing naturally too.
``Oh, really~?@k@|@y@vS01/02/120200179. And did you eat all of them, I wonder? ...I wonder?``
My faint smile froze once again.
...You managed to avoid swallowing the sewing needle?@k Was that what she was asking...?
If I'd swallowed it, there was no way I'd be standing here right now.
``N-No... I couldn't... eat all of them.@k@vS01/01/120100366. ......There are still some left.``
I was terrified... but that's how I deflected it.
``...Huh?@k@vS01/02/120200181. What about the homework to figure out which one Rena made...?``
``Ahahahaha... that homework... was it due today?``
``Yep. It's due today.@k@vS01/02/120200183. ...Mii-chan's going to be mad~ I bet there'll be a penalty game.``
We laughed together again.
To anyone watching, it was a perfectly ordinary morning scene.
Even I, if I let my guard down for just a moment... could almost believe it was just another normal morning...
But... I must never make that mistake.
Even this Rena, laughing so freely, had something lurking within her that her outward appearance could never suggest.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: I recalled that piercing voice -- one I could hardly believe had come from Rena's throat.
The instant I remembered that, something cold and viscous slithered down my back.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Was it only in that moment that something evil had possessed Rena...?
No, that was wrong. That was also Rena. Ooishi-san had said it himself, hadn't he...?

``Actually, when I looked into it, it turns out Ryuugu-san received a suspension from school shortly before she moved.@k@vS01/11/120700281. Apparently she went around the entire school smashing the windows.``
Rena possessed a darkness... far beyond anything a normal person could comprehend.@k No matter how brightly she smiled... that fact remained unchanged.
But... I couldn't begin to imagine what state she'd been in as she committed that rampage, smashing every window in the school...
One thing was certain: it hadn't been a brief, momentary impulse.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Breaking a single pane of glass in a flash of sudden anger... well, that might happen.
But every window in the entire school.
Just imagine yourself carrying a bat through your own school, smashing the windows.
One pane, then two, swinging with all your strength. Paying no heed to the shards flying everywhere...
Your classmates standing there, dumbstruck.
...Unable to move a muscle in the face of what was happening.
Where would the most windows be, lined up in a long row?@k ...Probably the hallway.
*Smash.* Walk. Wind up.
It was almost impossible to reconcile that terrifying scene with the Rena smiling beside me now...
But... even so, I had to imagine it.
......'Impossible because I can't imagine it' -- that kind of childish reasoning... no longer held any weight.
Rena, advancing steadily toward me, the air filled with the jarring sound of shattering glass, shards crunching beneath her feet.
......Her classmates, turning pale, forgetting even to breathe.
What had they done as Rena approached, smashing windows as she came closer?
Did they desperately try to talk her down, to reason with her?
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Or did they throw themselves at her, trying to physically stop her rampage?
Or did they run to the staff room to call for the teachers?
Probably none of those.
Faced with that chilling spectacle of Rena destroying window after window... all they could do was silently clear a path.
Standing there in shock... stepping aside for Rena's procession.
...To condemn them for looking the other way would be too harsh.
They weren't merely turning a blind eye.@k ......They knew it was the only way to protect themselves.
If someone did something different from the rest... Rena's interest might suddenly shift to them.
And what would Rena do to whoever caught her attention...?
The answer was simple.@k ......She would just act on her impulse, same as before.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...In other words, that person -- I -- would become the next window.
Rena advancing toward me, her eyes locked on mine, glass shards crunching underfoot with each step.
......I too was drawn into her gaze, unable to move a single muscle.
Then Rena struck me with the bat, over and over, just as she had the other windows.
I curled up on the floor, desperately shielding my head.@k Rena didn't care -- head, back, wherever.@k She just kept swinging, again and again.
What kind of expression was Rena wearing as she did this, over and over?
...I stole a glance upward.
......Her expression was utterly blank, and she looked deeply displeased.
Because no matter how many times she struck me, I didn't make the same satisfying sound as the glass.

Over and over.@k She kept striking.@k I wouldn't make the sound Rena wanted.@k The surrounding classmates didn't intervene.@k And she wouldn't move on to the next window until the current one was done.
Somebody help me...@k ...You all just look the other way unless we're having fun together...?!
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...Well, of course -- class rankings were a competition, after all.@k ...Nobody had anything to gain by helping a cram-school junkie like me...
Eventually, mixed in with a faint cracking sound -- like splitting a walnut shell -- a spray of something neither quite red nor black would begin to scatter...
At any rate, Rena's behavior couldn't have been something as momentary as losing herself in a fit of anger.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ......Taking a deep breath to still my racing heart, I recalled Ooishi-san's words more carefully.
changeReason: Old translation contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new translation accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: ``...And you see, in the medical records from her counseling sessions, the doctor recorded everything Rena said......@k@vS01/11/120700284. And there's something quite interesting in there.``
``What is it...?``
``It comes up.@k@|@y@vS01/11/120700288. The word 'Oyashiro-sama.'``
After that, Rena was suspended and began attending regular examinations at the hospital.
There, during her counseling sessions with the doctor, she said it over and over.@k 'Oyashiro-sama.'

``She claimed that this Oyashiro-sama -- some kind of ghostly being -- would come to her house night after night.@k@vS01/11/120700290. And it would scratch at her -- scritch, scritch, scritch -- she said.``
Since it was only part of her conversation with the doctor, I couldn't grasp the full picture... but it was far from a comforting tale.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: So then, was Rena... confessing that her rampage was because she'd been possessed by Oyashiro-sama...?
Until now, I hadn't wanted to acknowledge the curse of Oyashiro-sama.
That's why I'd tried to convince myself the mysterious deaths each year were the work of human culprits.
And the more I spoke with Ooishi-san, the more certain I became that the incidents had been perpetrated by human hands, not a curse.
But......if it was people behind the incidents... then everyone around me was deeply involved.
The price of refusing to believe in the curse was, of all things... acknowledging that my closest friends were deeply entangled in those cases.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Why?@k How?@k For what purpose?@k ...Rena?@k ...Everyone??
That was far more painful and difficult to accept than simply acknowledging that Oyashiro-sama's curse was real.
And yet, after her rampage, Rena had told the doctor at the hospital that it was because Oyashiro-sama had possessed her.
I felt a strange sense of relief at that.
So that's how it was.
There was no hidden side to Rena.
......It was precisely because she'd been possessed by something sinister called Oyashiro-sama... that she'd done such a thing.
It wasn't Rena's fault.@k Oyashiro-sama was to blame...!
...I knew.
I had it all backwards.

Refusing to believe in the curse, I'd desperately wanted a human culprit.@k......Now that suspicion had fallen on my closest friends, I was conveniently changing my tune, ready to blame Oyashiro-sama instead.
Which was the lesser evil? Accepting that Oyashiro-sama's curse was real, or accepting that Rena and the others might be deeply involved in the chain of mysterious deaths?
I didn't want to think about it.
I wanted to believe that by not thinking about it, I could preserve these ordinary days as they were.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: But......that was no longer possible.
I had already received their message.
...It was pathetic, the way I kept trying to twist the meaning to suit myself.
Whether my enemy was human or a curse.@k ......I would not be killed.
Like hell I'd let them kill me!@k ......Not without even knowing why...!
``Keiichi-kun?@k@vS01/02/120200186. ...You've been making a strange face for a while now.@k@vS01/02/120200187. Are you sure you're okay? ...Are you sure?``
Rena's voice startled me back to reality.
Before I knew it, we were already at the school entrance.
I shook my head a few times, dispelling the horrifying fantasies that had consumed my thoughts until just moments ago.
No matter how you looked at it... there was no way Rena would do something so terrifying......!
...But even as I thought it, the assertion had a feeble quality, as though I were merely trying to convince myself...
As I slid the classroom door open, a blackboard eraser loaded with chalk dust dropped onto my head.
Chalk powder got in my eyes, causing a brief moment of suffering.
``Ohohohoho!@k@vS01/04/120400054. How fitting for Keiichi-san, the truant~!``
``Good morning, Keiichi and Rena.``
``Gooood morning~! Satoko-chan, Rika-chan~!``
Not quite feeling up to it, I barely reacted to Satoko's trap.
Satoko had braced herself as I passed by, expecting some sort of retaliation, but since I just walked past without a word, she seemed deflated.
``Wh-What's with him...?@k@vS01/04/120400056. He's no fun at all.``
``...It seems Keiichi still isn't back to his usual self.``
``Yeah.@k@vS01/02/120200190. ......So go easy on him today, okay?``
Suddenly, my shoulder was slapped with a sharp smack. It stung a little...
``Yo! Kei-chan, did you get enough rest?``
It was Mion.
My head had been full of thoughts about Rena... but Mion was just as much a part of this.
Remember, Keiichi.@k ...That hawkish glare from yesterday.
``What's the matter, what's the matter, that's one lifeless greeting~?@k@vS01/03/120300124. Did you eat the ohagi we brought you?``
changeReason: New translation corrects 'mochi' to 'ohagi,' naming the specific Japanese confection in the scene.

TextENGNew: I'm like this because I did eat them...@k The words nearly crawled out of my throat.
``...I just didn't have much appetite.@k@vS01/01/120100371. ...I ate a few, but I ended up leaving most of them.``
``Hm? Then what about the homework?@k@vS01/03/120300126. Your answer on which one was Rena's homemade one?``
``Keiichi-kun forgot his homework.@k@vS01/02/120200192. Ahahahaha.``
``...Well, well... can't be helped then, time for a penalty game.@k@vS01/03/120300128. ...Heh heh heh heh!``
Flashing a sly grin, Mion returned to her seat.
None of our classmates found anything suspicious about the exchange.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Of course they didn't.
Anyone listening to our conversations this morning...@k ...would find absolutely nothing out of the ordinary.
And that was precisely why... it was so terrifying.
No matter what happened to an individual named Maebara Keiichi, their behavior was so impeccable that no one would ever suspect a thing... and that, inversely, was what frightened me.
Before long, the teacher arrived.
She asked about my condition, took attendance, and the same dull routine resumed.
